mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of AI models while optimizing performance through intelligent load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential complexity in model management.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeAI Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_inference_crd.yaml

```

> Installs the Model CRD.
> Installs the Inference CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeAI operator in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI in the kubeai namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeAI pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists all pods in the kubeai namespace to verify the installation.
> Verifies that the CRDs are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if models or configurations are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> Uninstalls the KubeAI Helm release.
> Deletes the kubeai namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step will upgrade KubeAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl uncordon <pod-name>

```

> Drains the KubeAI pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Uncordons the pods after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kubeai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD installation may have failed due to network issues or incorrect URLs.

**Fix:**
Re-run the CRD installation commands and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the model specifications.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get pods returns no resources found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** KubeAI may not be installed correctly or the namespace may not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure KubeAI is installed and check the namespace with 'kubectl get namespaces'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the Kubernetes cluster or kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure your Kubernetes cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kubeai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements for optimal performance.
